goal: Embedded forms should automatically append the country code when a C2 enters a 10 digit number
constraints: Embedded forms currently require manual entry of the country code. Pop-up forms already auto-detect and pre-fill the country flag and code. This inconsistency is causing signup drop-off and confusion for fans on live C3/Live Nation event sites where embedded forms are in use. The gap was confirmed by Solutions Consulting and is a known parity issue. As C3 consulting expands across the Live Nation portfolio, this friction point will surface repeatedly across new properties.
workaround: No effective workaround exists. Customers can switch to pop-up forms, but that is not always viable given site design requirements for embedded placements. The only other option is adding instructional copy telling users to manually enter +1, which is a poor experience and still results in errors.

goal: Custom Reports is currently pulling all-time revenue for certain automations, causing a spike in reported revenue. For HC Brands, this means their automation revenue is being inaccurately aggregated on March 3. This has been escalated to Engineering and PM, but the customer is frustrated and looking for a fix ASAP and has followed up for an update. I’ll include Sol Con threads and the PM folks who have been helping here. Thanks for your help!

As of last Friday, this customer has been seeing issues with their email campaigns sending via Mandrill/TX. They mentioned receiving 404s for some 40k messages last Friday. The workflow is that they send a big wave of emails at the start of the month to all users, so it’s typically their highest email volume. After sending the emails, which by all evidence appeared to be successful, they were unable to fetch the message content back from Mandrill to store a copy on their side (legal requirement), instead receiving 404 responses. They have tried repeatedly to fetch the messages from Mandril to no avail. Please see a handful of example message IDs below.

741be645c6d14d8d8655dbeb697ab04e
201c3f8f5937413f86cadde31c129dc5
947d3e11ae174e1bb3dc0dea5a7ade0c
a8ed63865a384df3b351a6c1139e55fa
8633914abc1b4782838f2121753bc494

To provide some more specifics, the 404s are from POST messages/content.json that we use to fetch the rendered email HTML after sending has finished. Sometimes there is a delay between sending the message and being able to retrieve it back from this endpoint, but usually succeeds after a few retries. The problem initially started around 11:30am EDT on Friday. I have just tried to re-drive the 40k messages, still seeing the 404s, so you should see new logs coming in.

This account belongs to Shoval on the EMEA Team, so will CC her coverage.
Prevent duplicate opt-out language in SMS messages when a marketer has already manually included compliance text in the message body.
constraints: The checkbox currently defaults to ON. When an associate manually includes opt-out text in the message body (copy/paste workflows) and forgets to uncheck the option, the opt-out language appears twice in the sent message — this happened during the Darker Waves live announcement this week.
workaround: Associates must remember to manually switch toggle OFF each time they compose an SMS that already contains opt-out language. Rating: Moderately Painful (human-error-prone; every send requires an extra manual step that is easy to forget under time pressure).

goal: Add validation that prevents saving an Opt-in Request message if the required "reply Y" confirmation prompt language is missing. If the message can't be fully locked, customer wants at minimum an error or warning when a user attempts to save an Opt-in Request message without the compliance-required reply language.
constraints:
The SMS automation editor has no content validation for compliance-critical fields. A user can save any arbitrary text as the Opt-in Request message — including copy that omits the "reply Y to receive updates" prompt — without any warning. 

Broken DOI flows are left to be discovered after the fact when customer sees anomalous reporting
workaround: Manual auditing of the Opt-in Request message after any changes to SMS automations. No system-generated alert or audit log is surfaced when this message is modified. Rating: Painful.

goal: Prevent accidental modification of the SMS "Opt-in Request" message. Customer wants this compliance-critical message locked or restricted so marketing team members cannot inadvertently break the double opt-in (DOI) flow by editing the wrong message.
constraints:
Any user with access to SMS automations can freely edit the Opt-in Request message with no guardrails. There is no distinction between this compliance-critical message and general marketing copy. 

On 3/24/2026, a marketing associate changed the Opt-in Request instead of the Opt-in Confirmation, causing all new SMS subscribers (~4+ hours during a major festival lineup drop) to get stuck in "Pending" status. Resolution required an engineering-assisted script — customer cannot self-serve due to DOI compliance restrictions on moving pending contacts to subscribed.
workaround: Internal team training and manual vigilance. If the mistake occurs, recovery requires engineering intervention with a custom script. Rating: Painful / High Effort.

goal:
Number discrepancy between Custom Reports & Campaign View

Goal: Have a single, reliable source of truth for campaign performance metrics, particularly unsubscribes and bounces, regardless of where C1 accesses the data in Mailchimp.
constraints: The numbers in C1's custom reports don't match what she sees when she clicks into the individual campaign and views the report there. It's unclear whether this is a timing/refresh issue, a reporting window difference, or a bug.
workaround: C1 has to manually compare both views and try to reconcile the numbers herself, which is time-consuming and undermines confidence in the data.

goal:
Delivery Rate displaying as 100% in reports when it isn't.

C1 wants to see accurate, precise delivery rate percentages so they can genuinely assess deliverability health. To the C1, 100% means zero bounces, so the rounding creates a misleading signal.

"100% to me, means there's no bounces...it's also like not true...all the rates ,I think, should be the same -- it should be like 99.62%, like open rate, 7.4%. The points is what I'm trying to say would be helpful to have in every single rate that's calculated just so that it can be consistent."
constraints: The product appears to round any rate above 99.5% up to 100%, and drops decimal places entirely, which is inconsistent with how other metrics like open rate are displayed.
workaround: C1 can manually calculate the true delivery rate from the raw numbers in the export, but that's an extra step that defeats the purpose of having the metric surfaced in the UI (in their own words).

goal: Export A/B test performance data that includes the subject line tested, so C1s can analyze results without having to cross-reference multiple places.
constraints: The export only labels campaigns as "Group A" and "Group B" with no subject line included, making the data largely unusable on its own for subject line tests.
workaround: A custom API report includes subject lines, so that partially covers the need. However, it requires manually rerunning the report and is an interim solution, not a native product fix. Moderately effective but not self-serve.

goal: Quickly view the HTML version of a previously sent A/B test email directly from the campaign list, the same way you'd preview a regular campaign.
constraints: There's no direct "preview" button for sent A/B tests. The interface defaults to plain text and the HTML view is buried under "Details." C1 also noted she forgets where it lives between uses, which suggests it's not a natural path.
workaround:
Go to the campaign, click "Details," and retrieve the URL from there. 

It works, but it's unintuitive and easy to forget, making it a moderately painful workaround for something that could be one click away.

goal: add "Cease" and/or "Cease communication" to SMS compliance keywords.
constraints: The customer has received a pending threat of a lawsuit due to customers responding "cease" or "cease communication" to SMS messages and not being opted out.
workaround: No present workaround. The customer has stated the potential to churn if we aren't able to add these compliances words/phrase.

goal: Netflix is requesting ability to see how many campaigns are being created and sent by individual users. Netflix wants to track the sender email and how many campaigns are associated with a specific sender. C1 would like to see this data over time to distinguish between a drop in platform utility vs a natural lull in marketing calendar.
constraints: There are no reporting by sender (user) metrics found within Mailchimp UI.
workaround: Yes, the campaigns by sender name can be exposed via the API. C1 noted that another internal team has built a Tableau dashboard via data exports, but our contact is exploring if this level of detail can be accessed directly within Mailchimp to avoid a complex build-out.

goal: Audience Import
constraints: Unable to import the whole audience due to the Jersey Phone number issue.
workaround: NA

goal: Over on Beehive and Bigmailer we can create segments based on whether a User has Clicked X times in X Days. I cannot describe how useful it would be to have this ability on our Mailchimp RSS sends.  Please see the Wish list Image.
constraints: Refined targeting based off of campaign interaction
workaround: N/A

goal: When creating a  regular segment, it looks like I can target any number of "campaigns". Would it be possible to have the same ability for "days"?
constraints: Having agency over number of days instead of having to leverage the preloaded options.
workaround: N/A

goal: C1 wants to confirm that bulk tagging operation is complete.
constraints:
C1 is seeing two different counts, depending on where they access tags from: 

First: <https://us12.admin.mailchimp.com/audience/contacts>
C1 starts from the audience contacts table > selects "tags" dropdown > chooses `VIP23-24_20260310` > sees `19,765` contacts

Second: <https://us12.admin.mailchimp.com/audience/tags/>
C1 starts from audience tags page > clicks "view" button for `VIP23-24_20260310` > sees `19,796` contacts

There is a `31` contact difference between those two counts depending on where the C1 starts from.

goal:
At-risk (CAM activated) account wants a Mailchimp sandbox to trial with their Salesforce sandbox.

Direct quote:
"I just met with Lynn to discuss the MC/Sf connector and we're going to pick back up on exploring the possibility of implementing the connector. We have the MC connector in our Salesforce sandbox—is it possible to have a Mailchimp sandbox to do some testing?"
constraints: N/A
workaround: N/A

goal: User requesting ability to see Segment performance in custom reporting.
constraints: Not natively available.
workaround: Suggested workaround of changing naming convention for campaigns being sent to specific Segments and then sorting by campaign/campaign type in Custom Reports.

goal: The customer has an audience that is roughly 60% Spanish speaking and finds the lack of translation for compliance keywords limiting.
constraints: Spanish speaking customer are responding "detener" to unsubscribe from SMS and because of the lack of translation they have received a few abuse reports.
workaround: There is no workaround.

goal:
They need to send SMS at 7am Sydney time (AEDT and AEST) on weekdays.

Mailchimp’s current SMS quiet hour guardrails prevent sends before 9am local time in AU, and quiet hours can’t currently be configured earlier at the account level by the customer. We suspect the reason 9am is set as the earliest allowable send is because it’s the most conservative blanket time across Australia’s multiple time zones.

From a compliance perspective:
The Spam Act 2003 (AU) does not prescribe specific prohibited send hours - it requires consent, sender ID, and unsubscribe.
“Reasonable hours” is the guiding principle.
In Sydney Tools’ case, the vast majority of their audience are tradespeople who start work before 7am on weekdays, so a 7am (local time for receiver - state based) weekday send would reasonably align with when their customers are active.
Sydney Tools are comfortable sending at 9am on weekends, so this would apply to weekdays only.

Given that context, please assist us in
Broadening/customising quiet hours for this account (e.g., allow weekday sends from 7am local time)?
I.e. A controlled way to enable pre-9am weekday sends without triggering compliance flagging (e.g., account-level exemption with documented business justification)?
constraints: Mailchimp’s current SMS quiet hour guardrails prevent sends before 9am local time in AU, and quiet hours can’t currently be configured earlier at the account level by the customer. We suspect the reason 9am is set as the earliest allowable send is because it’s the most conservative blanket time across Australia’s multiple time zones.
workaround: None right now.

goal:
Enable "Segment-within-Segments" (Nesting) and "Set Math" (Unions, Intersections, and Exclusions) within the segment builder. Users need to be able to reference existing segments as criteria for new ones, rather than rebuilding complex logic for every new audience.

The Goal
Targeting Precision: Easily identify the "Venn Diagram" of fans—for example, people who are in the "2024 VIP Buyer" segment AND the "2025 GA Buyer" segment.

Reduced Manual Labor: Eliminate the need to manually export/import CSVs to handle exclusions or to verify audience overlaps.

Intuitive UX: Create a "mathematical" approach to segmentation that aligns with how marketers think (e.g., “Include Audience A, but exclude anyone in Audience B”).
constraints:
Real-Time Syncing: Nested segments would have to update dynamically. If a user enters "Segment A," they should automatically appear in any "Parent" segment that references Segment A.

Cross-Entity Support: Logic should support event-level data (e.g., ticket types like "VIP" vs "GA") tied to specific years or festivals.

UI Visualization: Provide a "count" or "reach" estimate during the segment building phase so users can see how many people fall into the overlap before hitting save.
workaround: Rebuilding Logic: Marketers have to recreate 5+ layers of criteria (Location + Purchase History + Open Rate) for every new segment because they cannot simply click "Exclude [Existing Segment X]."

goal:
Core Need: Implement an automated Global Frequency Cap to manage marketing pressure during high-volume periods (e.g., "Announcement Season").

The primary objective is to maintain a premium fan experience by preventing "Inbox Fatigue." During festival lineups, fans often qualify for multiple segments (VIP, GA, Past Buyer). Without a cap, they receive redundant communications, leading to higher unsubscribe rates and brand damage. Automation is required to replace the current manual, error-prone exclusion workflows.
constraints: This doesn't currently exist, and they are having to do it manually.
workaround:
Manual Exclusions: Staff must manually create a dynamic segment of users who received any communication in the last 24 hours.

Workflow Drag: This "Exclude" segment has to be manually attached to every individual campaign, which is unsustainable during peak season and prone to human error.

goal: ability to label contacts as VIP, and they get prioritized for all sends (email or sms)
constraints: VIPs on their list aren't receiving the SMS campaigns in a timely manner.
workaround: tagging VIPs, and creating two separate sends. They expressed that this would be too much effort to be worth it.

goal: use the chatgpt x Mailchimp connector to review analytics within chatgpt
constraints: the connector doesn't expose Mailchimp data, so it can't read campaigns or analyze data
workaround: exporting the data from mailchimp and importing into chatgpt, relying on analytics agent, which still has room for improvement in it's current state.

goal: The C1 wants the SMS campaign progress bar to reflect Handoff Success (messages successfully accepted by the gateway/Infobip) as the primary completion metric, rather than waiting for Delivery Receipts (DLRs) from carriers.
constraints: Mailchimp's current "Completion" logic is tied to DLR reconciliation. In high-volume festival sends, carriers often delay DLRs for the final 4–5% of a list. This causes the UI to sit at around "95% Sending" for an hour or more, leading the customer to believe the broadcast has stalled when the messages have actually already left the platform.
workaround:
Customer manually audits internal devices or contacts CSM/Engineering to verify if the "stall" is just a reporting lag. 

High Effort / High Anxiety

goal: Fivetran is attempting to sync Mailchimp data for our mutual customer, C3 Presents, into a Databricks datalake. Specifically, they are using the Marketing API to retrieve a list of members within a specific segment (/lists/{list_id}/segments/{segment_id}/members) to maintain automated data tables and enable advanced segmentation features.
constraints: The primary constraint is a persistent HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout (originating as an Akamai "first byte timeout") specifically for a large segment on shard us12. While other requests pass, this specific list-segment pair triggers an application-level failure where the segmentation service fails to reach the CDP within the 120-second timeout window.
workaround: There is currently no definitive workaround that resolves the core timeout. Initial suggestions to implement per-shard throttling (15 req/s) and back-off strategies were ineffective because the root cause is an application-side connection failure, not rate limiting. While allow-listing production IPs was performed, the 504 errors persist for this specific dataset.

goal: Customer is using timewarp, and is finding that the campaigns are sending earlier than the scheduled date.
constraints: campaigns are sending out at the wrong time for certain subscribers.
workaround: N/A

goal: Creative Fabrica (UID: 48624181) is trying to fetch campaign data from the Mailchimp /reports endpoint via their self-hosted Airbyte ETL tool. While other tables (lists, tags, campaigns) sync successfully, they are attempting to pull large batches of detailed reporting data into their data warehouse for analysis.
constraints: The customer is hit by consistent HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout errors specifically on the /reports table. The process successfully retrieves initial pages but consistently times out and is severed by the gateway after reaching approximately 3,000 records. This suggests a processing bottleneck or backend latency specifically when compiling large batches of campaign report data.
workaround: There is no currently identified workaround for the automated ETL process. While users can likely view individual reports in the UI, the automated data sync is blocked. Attempting to use the reports-search method is unavailable due to their self-hosted Airbyte configuration. The issue is high-pain as it stops the flow of reporting data, and investigation is ongoing to see if a retry or smaller batching is possible.

goal: Option for a Mailchimp form that would open up upon clicking instead of popping up on its own
constraints: Right now, our forms are embedded or they show up when someone lands on a page and meets the criteria
workaround: No

goal: The customer is facing issues with segments loading incorrect numbers and also reporting for delivery taking much longer

The customer needs to use New Custom Reports to track A/B testing performance across multiple concurrent campaigns to optimize subject lines and CTAs for upcoming event. 

They are specifically trying to correlate "Winner," "Group A," and "Group B" metrics with their respective parent campaign titles to ensure data accuracy for quarterly performance reviews.
constraints:
The reporting interface disaggregates the data, showing the campaign title on a line with zeroed-out metrics while displaying the actual opens and clicks on separate lines that lack the identifying title. 

Since the customer runs multiple tests simultaneously, they cannot rely on send times to manually match these disparate data points, and the redundant "zeroed" lines are skewing their overall performance averages.
workaround: The current workaround involves manually exporting data to a CSV and attempting to re-associate titles with their metrics, or creating separate reports for A/B versus regular campaigns to isolate the flawed data. This process is considered extremely painful and high-labor as it requires significant manual cleanup to make the reporting comprehensible for stakeholders.

goal: C1 is currently undergoing a rebranding and making template updates. Because their tokens are shared at the account level rather than the audience level, multiple teams managing unique audiences within Mailchimp are struggling to coordinate within the current 25-token limit.
constraints: Not enough tokens to test as desired, particularly for multiple users/audiences in one account.
workaround: According to the KB, if you run out of tokens for the month but need more previews, you can sign up for a Litmus account. All Mailchimp users receive their first 7 days of Litmus for free, but it's paid after that.

goal: When Microsoft was having delivery issues back in January, a bunch of emails bounced for The Baltimore Banner. They believe that these emails bounced erroneously and would like to bring them back into the audience. For them, this is crucial because these contacts are paid subscribers who are looking to receive their daily updates.
constraints: There is no way for our client to unbounce contacts in app if they bounced due to some provider error (such as Microsoft). They want to maintain the date subscribed field as is and not reconfirm or resubscribe them which would mess with their data.
workaround: Our team does have a tool to unbounce contacts. but for some reason it's not working for the list of contacts that they had sent over. Our team has tried unbouncing but we're not seeing the contacts update in app. Attached is the list The Baltimore Banner sent over for us to review of all the contacts they think should be unbounced/uncleaned.

goal: Customer is trying to customize the GA Title for emails but is unable to save the GA Title for certain emails.

goal: Enable Netflix to collaborate with FTEs and agency partners in Mailchimp while ensuring users only have access to the content that’s relevant to their role, region, or team (e.g. preventing agency partners from seeing campaigns for other markets).
constraints: Mailchimp’s current global setup does not support granular role-based access controls. As a result, agency users can sometimes see content outside of their intended scope For example, a partner in Brazil having visibility into upcoming US releases. This creates a potential security risk, especially for global teams managing sensitive or market-specific content.
workaround: Netflix is currently accepting this risk by limiting access to trusted agency partners and by relying on the high volume of content in the account to reduce accidental exposure. However, more granular role-based access controls (e.g. by audience, folder, campaign type, or team) would significantly reduce this risk while still supporting cross-functional collaboration.

goal: They'd like the ability to have a different reply to address than the sending address
constraints: this can not be changed outside of using inbox
workaround: using inbox.

goal:
The customer operates in a multitude of countries, some where SMS is available (Spain, Germany), some that are coming (Poland), some where it isn't and aren't coming imminently (Mexico, Colombia).
They want to be able to send all their contacts, including phone numbers in the SMS phone number field, regardless of country, so that when a new country becomes available in SMS, the data is already there and the number isn't 'lost'
constraints:
The customer tested SMS with Spain, when they set up the API, the were sending contacts into MC with both email and 'SMS phone numbers' (these SMS phone numbers were for all the countries they operate in)
Since some of the numbers were for countries that were not contracted or supported, those SMS phone numbers were not recorded (they flagged in some cases also weren't being recorded, despite having email addresses, but no example has been provided, as this happened in November before the account was handed over to me). This caused them to suspend their trial and  which means that when they open new countries
workaround:
Recording the number twice, in the SMS field and the Phone Number field that way the numnber isn't "lost" but when they open a market they would have to move them to the other field manually.

This was one of the reasons why, after testing, they didn't go ahead with SMS for the regions we support

goal: Request for MVT Campaigns to reflect bot filtering in the All Campaigns view
constraints: Unable to access accurate email campaign reporting
workaround: N/A

goal: customer would like to consolidate accounts into one master account, but needs to be able to have control over what audiences each user within the consolidated account is able to access.
constraints: They are working out of separate Mailchimp accounts right now, and are wanting to consolidate for billing purposes and ease of use, but need this functionality in order to consolidate due to sensitive information across the different accounts
workaround: remain working out of separate Mailchimp accounts.

goal: Customer needs to load reports, but it's taking a long time. I will attach a video.
constraints: Cannot access data in a timely manner - have to wait around and check back later.

goal:
Directly from the client related to Bot Filtering: The email reports we have been reviewing seem to be very off from what we are used to seeing. We are also noticing that the reports refer to "open" rate in several places but show different numbers for the same email. We really need to have solid reporting so that our clients don't get suspicious/non trusting of the report we provide to them. This has already become somewhat of a delay in revenue in terms of 2026 campaigns. We desperately need answers and adjustments if necessary to remove bot filtering that seems to be way to aggressive and not accurate in many cases.

It would have been better if Mailchimp would have sent out a notification sharing what they were looking to do and offer to answer any questions/concerns before just going ahead and changing the platform. Our contract is based on the reporting that we expected and have always gotten
constraints: MVT Campaigns do not reflect bot filtering in the All Campaigns view. While the open rate has bot filtering included for normal campaigns, when you scroll down to the benchmarking another open rate is shown. Open rates per email domain also are not reflective of bot filtering. When you choose to export the csv from the page, it shows an open rate without bot filtering.
workaround: Turn off bot filtering and don't use it if you want consistent numbers in all reporting

goal: Customer would like to have granular permissions in that they want the owner to set access per user per audience. They would also like the ability to change that on an as needed basis. For example, they will send missionaries to a college to serve there. There is a team of 5 and one leader. They want the account to be under the college campus site, but then the team of 5 and one leader all have access to it to send out their donor emails. They want the one leader to see everyone's emails, but everyone else should only see their emails.
constraints: This is not possible with our current capabilities
workaround: Everyone is designated with their own account, but this makes it hard to add staff as the year goes on because they are on an annual plan for a certain allotment of accounts (Annual Payment of about $60,000)

goal:
C1 reached out to me for assistance in downloading a performance report for a Classic Automation that has been archived.

Audience: SealSkinCovers Customer List
Automation name: Promo Code Workflow (Updated 032620) - <https://us14.admin.mailchimp.com/reports/summary?id=324850>

They are trying to extract a daily performance report for the past 30 days for this Classic Automation. They attempted to generate a custom report, but it fails every time. They are asking if we can generate the report to share with them.

- Is there a way they can download day-wise performance data for any Classic Automation?
- Is there a recommended or supported method to extract this data?

For context, they find it much easier to generate daily reports in Customer Journeys, and are currently in the process of migrating from Classic Automations to Customer Journeys. As part of this evaluation, they want to compare performance and therefore need daily reports from Classic Automations.
constraints: Cannot view this data in Custom Reports. Daily performance not accessible in Classic Automations

Have bot filtering (Apple MPP & bot clicks) visibility on A/B and multivariate test campaigns. 

C1 asked me for clarity on whether bot filtering would be added to the A/B testing suite so that their data would match across all campaign types.
constraints: C1 pointed out that they were seeing "vastly different" click rates and click-to-open rates depending on whether an email was part of an A/B test. He realized that this was because the new bot filtering feature was active for regular campaigns but notably absent from A/B tests.

goal: Architecture Media is trying to accurately measure engagement  (click activity) with their email newsletters so they can reliably assess content performance and report the results to their advertisers. They need confidence that reported clicks reflect real subscriber interest, not inflated activity from bots.
constraints: The data is displaying correctly in the campaign overview but it is not filtering out the bot activity on the click performance tab. The customer needs to be able to quantify the clicks for the specific URLs so they can report this back to their advertisers.

goal: Trying to get accurate reporting from date ranges within the flow report.
constraints: When filtering for specific date ranges, metrics such as open rate and delivery rate do not reflect their performance for the selected range, rather their performance related to the entire history of the flow. Example in screenshots, when I select a date range in the flow report of Jan 10-13, it shows a 45.5% delivery rate when the true delivery rate for the period is 98.4% (8569 deliveries / 8704 sends)
workaround: Manually calculating the true rate using the total numbers, while accurate, this is tedious and extremely time consuming when wanting to look at and compare multiple date ranges in a flow.

They requested the ability to select custom date ranges for any previous year (e.g., all of 2023 or 2024, etc.) to perform year-over-year performance comparisons.

C1 needs to compare different types of emails from years past to identify long-term trends, a feature they noted is available in other ESPs.

goal: Customer has bot filtering on, but has noticed that when they go into the click reporting, all the contacts still show reported clicks per email and it's not filtered for bot clicks.

workaround: No - they want to see all bot clicks filtered out and then tie the not bot clicks back to certain email addresses. This doesn't seem to be possible right now.

goal: use the title attribute of the link in the segment builder

goal: In audience segmentation, the C1 would like to have the "Contains" option for the "Event Properties"
constraints: We've already added "Contains" to the "Event Name", so this is an expansion of that work.

constraints: customer is having to pause sending, and reach out to us to reset the testing limit each time
workaround: currently the only option is to have it reset each time at the campaign level

goal: Would like to gain the ability to schedule recurring RSS campaigns to be sent at a specific time rather that at the top of the hour.
constraints: The tool currently doesnt allow for them to select any given time and their research has annotated that ISPs are prone to sending them to spam when they send 7 campaigns all at the same time and because they have so many campaigns all on MailChimp, this limitation is highly problematic for them. For them, it would be quite high priority.
workaround: N/A that I know of

goal: Customer would like to be able to select an exact time to send an RSS campaign as opposed to the top of the hour. (e.g. 4:13pm versus 4pm)

C1 uses SlickText to send ~5,000-50,000 SMS messages per week. They want to use Mailchimp for SMS but is having trouble with this use case.

Medical Solutions needs to trigger an internal email to a salesperson (recruiter) when a customer responds to an SMS campaign, including the customer's response and details.
constraints: Mailchimp does not have a direct SMS response trigger; responses appear in the Mailchimp Inbox and require manual intervention.
workaround:
After manually tagging contacts in the Mailchimp Inbox, an automation with the starting point "tag is added" could be set up, and then a webhook could be configured to notify recruiters externally.

This is still a manual process and is not ideal.

goal: New data contact has joined TP and would like to know if we support FTP or auto-FTP uploads.
constraints: They have been having issues with uploads "crapping out" after 50k contacts. I am trying to set-up a call with their new data contact and an SC/Engineer on out end.
workaround: They are a big fan of FTP and asking we support it. Is FTP on our roadmap or do we work with 3rd-part apps that support FTP imports into MC?

goal:
Dynamic Sender Name for Portfolio Managers' 1:1 communication.

C1's team uses Pardot/Account Engagement to send mass emails where the "From Name" dynamically changes to the recipient's assigned portfolio manager (e.g., Matt or Paige). This ensures replies go directly to the correct manager, maintaining a personalized, high-value donor relationship.

Related to the above, they need a system to manage and assign responses that come back to the email (a feature that their portfolio managers rely on).

The existing Mailchimp Inbox is not structured to automatically route or filter replies to the respective portfolio manager (e.g., Matt only sees replies from his contacts).
constraints:
This functionality is currently missing in Mailchimp, which would require splitting the audience into multiple lists based solely on the portfolio manager. 

Routing responses to particular MC Inboxes isn't suppported as descrived
workaround: Split into multiple audiences/sends

goal: customer would like for the correct shortcode for the audience selected from the dropdown to display in the SMS Settings Overview section under Sender ID
constraints: Regardless of the audience selected from the dropdown, the number for the AWF audience is displaying under the Sender ID in the sending numbers section.
workaround: N/A

goal: Customer loves the new flow for A/B testing subject lines, and they would like to see this replicated for A/B testing of email content. Rather than having to start a multivariate email from the beginning, they want an easy option to A/B test a campaign when they're creating it through the normal flow
constraints: Right now, you would have to go to 'multivariate test' and build out the content A/B test that way.
workaround: Use the current MVT flow

goal: Request for "fallback mode" for MMS - where an SMS in sent if MMS delivery fails.
constraints: functionality does not exist - limited visibility into SMS bounce reason
workaround: We send them a report each week on Thursday. They then use that report to make changes to who they send an MMS to versus SMS.

goal: Ideal Image wants to create segments based on SMS bounce reason to better understand which C2s they should target with MMS vs SMS.
constraints: SMS bounce reason is unavailable in-app
workaround: We send them a report each week on Thursday. They then use that report to make changes to who they send an MMS to versus SMS.

goal:
C3 Presents is trying to reconcile and merge duplicate subscriber profiles in their Mailchimp audience, specifically addressing the separation of their existing email profiles and the imported SMS-only profiles that belong to the same individual.

The end goal is to achieve a single, unified customer profile per individual:

- A single profile would contain both the individual's email address and SMS phone number.
- This unified profile would give C3 a "full view of the customer".
- The merged profiles would unlock better segmentation capabilities for their marketers.
constraints:
The primary constraints are related to data limitations from the original export and the current state of Mailchimp's profile management tools:

1. Data Export Limitation: The initial export of SMS subscribers from their previous provider (Attentive) only contained phone numbers and no additional data. This lack of metadata made it difficult to link the new SMS profiles to existing email profiles.
2. Mailchimp System Architecture: When importing the new SMS contacts, if a phone number did not match an existing email address, a separate, new profile was created, resulting in subscribers having two different profiles (one for email, one for SMS). Mailchimp's native tools do not currently perform automatic, long-term profile reconciliation.
3. Third-Party Integration Concerns: C3 was initially offered assistance for contact migration from a third-party service (108 Degrees), but C3 became uncomfortable and declined due to "legal concerns over data & privacy with a third party".
workaround:
I'm not certain on any of these, but this is where my mind goes on this one: 

1. Engineering One-Time Operation (Initially offered to C3): Mailchimp engineers shared early on that they could perform a one-time, manual operation to run a script and reconcile duplicate profiles after the initial contact imports were completed and C3 began using the platform.
2. Utilizing Events API (Potential): C3 discussed that future integration of the Events API with transaction data (which contains both email and phone number from the ticket purchase) could help the system gather "signals" to eventually perform the profile reconciliation automatically.
3. Future In-App Reconciliation (Roadmap): The permanent solution is an in-app, automated reconciliation feature.

goal:
The customer, C3 Presents, needs to create and deploy sign-up forms (both evergreen and presale) for their various music festivals. A critical part of their business involves setting up these forms for new, unannounced festivals to support SMS short code applications and begin list collection.

This process must remain completely confidential to prevent leaking the new festival's name, branding, or URLs before an official public announcement.
constraints:
The current forms architecture (v1.5) fundamentally breaks our confidentiality requirement due to a significant information leak. The technical cause is that the v1.5 platform requires a single config file that includes all published forms to decide which one to render. 

This single JavaScript config file (e.g., 50561889.json) is currently publicly loaded on all of C3's festival websites. The "leak" is that this file contains a JSON object listing every published form, exposing sensitive data in plain text. This includes Festival & Form Names (like "FW Presale"), Confidential URLs (exposing domains for secret sites, e.g., railbirdfest.com), and Audience Metadata (which can contain the festival's name). This is a critical failure because we know our fans actively scan our code for this exact type of information to leak unannounced festivals.

I've attached the json file for visibility if anyone is interested
workaround:
1. Workaround Steps:
1.a. Avoid the New Form Builder: The customer completely avoids using the new form builder for any confidential festival setup.
1.b. Use Old Embedded Forms: They revert to using the "native MailChimp embed form" (the older, basic version), as it does not rely on the problematic JavaScript config file. They rely on the old embedded form for application & certification, then switch to the new forms when the festival is announced publicly and festival privacy is no longer a concern. 

2. Use the New Form Builder:
2.a. Obscure All Data: They must create new audiences using "fake" names or acronyms (e.g., "MCC" instead of "Minnesota Country Club"). They also manually change all list metadata, footers, and "from" addresses to hide the festival's identity.
2.b. Publish Only When Live: As advised by engineering, the only way to use the new builder is to keep forms in a 'Draft' state and only move them to 'Published' status the moment the festival site goes live, which doesn't work for their presale/short code application workflow.

goal: Customer wants to be able to pull SMS replies in the Inbox feature through an API endpoint. This is a critical part of their business as they reply on replies and conversation to nurture their leads and they need visibility into how the conversation is flowing.
constraints: Customer cannot do that right now so they set up a custom workaround but, in their words, it is 'clunky'
workaround: Custom coded a workaround

goal: Customer wants to see in app which numbers bounce from an MMS message. Some of those contacts can receive SMS still (depending on the message that was sent back from the bounce), so they want to target them and engage that way rather than leaving them out of all comms. Right now we cannot have them download in app the bounce reasons for an SMS or MMS.  They also want to be able to create segments based on bounce reasons for SMS so they can do some segmentation in app of who to send an MMS to versus SMS.
constraints: Cannot download their bounce reasons on their own time. They rely on us to pull a bounce report from InfoBip each week. You also cannot access bounce reasons or data within the segment builder at this time.
workaround: We send them a report each week on Thursday. They then use that report to make changes to who they send an MMS to versus SMS.

goal: Customer would like to see specific and granular insights when it comes to 'poor' reputation for a tag in Transactional. Though it highlights a timeframe, it does not tell you what exactly happened and potential steps to mitigate.
constraints: Customer has to do the research on their own and try to figure out what happened and what kind of responses the emails were getting (ex: are they bad emails or are the mailboxes full?)
workaround: Need to do your own research, but this can be cumbersome if you have multiple tags you need to look into

goal: User would like to be able to leverage pop ups for email capture within their app, and then have those sign ups sent into Mailchimp as subscribed contacts who they can email
constraints: right now, the pop ups are only available to display on websites.
workaround: N/A

goal: Client would like their button to have a transparent background but also have a border around it.
constraints: Client is unable to make transparent buttons right now (so that the button would show the color or image behind it)
workaround: You could create the design in Canva and put the image in Mailchimp with the desired effect

goal: Customer would like to have the ability in Nuni to add in new sections. Right now they are limited to Header, Body, Footer. The reason they want to do this is for them to add a background image (or content background) to a particular section and have text overlayed on top (as well as a CTA).
constraints: Right now you can accomplish setting a background image for header, body, and footer - but it is limited to those sections and you cannot create new ones.
workaround: You could use custom coding for it.

goal: Pull time series reporting for an older Flow (created before we released time series reporting). With this old flow, she paused it and did an A/B split to test two emails. Now she wants to see which email did better in the past month.
constraints: Cannot look at time series reporting.
workaround: You have to duplicate the flow and start it over again in order to get access to time series reporting.

goal: Gear Patrol wants to build emails in Mailchimp that utilize the RSS feed.
constraints: They avoid building emails in MC because of intermittent “No RSS items found” errors, and now rely on Feed Otter (MC partner) to stabilize that flow. They build emails in their CMS → Feed Otter → Mailchimp.
workaround: Use Feed Otter

goal: C1 would like to call the /audiences endpoint and see counts for "total_contacts", "sms_subscribers", and "email_subscribers"
constraints: Before the public beta, we were able to GET /audiences and receive a response with counts for total contacts, sms subscribers, and email subscribers. Now we are only getting the "total_contacts" count.
workaround: No workaround

goal:
User has raised this feedback in the past for timeframe flexibility within segmentation.

For example, they'd like to be able to select campaigns opened "within" a timeframe beyond the last 3 months (for example, the last 6 months, 9 months, etc). Even an open text field would work for their use case.
constraints: They can't use the "is after date" selector because it's static and they need a rolling/auto-adjusting segment condition
workaround: Would require manual updating if using "is after date" selector

goal: Customer wants to add device specific click report as an option to custom reports
constraints: The device specific click report is only available on the individual campaign level and there's no option for single report bulk export to create an overview for the audience.
workaround: painful to analyze campaign click reports on individual level

goal: Customer wants to add email domain performance and device specific click report as an option to custom reports.
constraints: The email domain performance and device specific click report are only available on the individual campaign level and there's no option for single report bulk export to create an overview for the audience.
workaround: unknown

goal: Customer wants to be able to set the background for text or button with an image - right now you can only set a background image for header, body, footer. they want to be able to do it for individual content blocks
constraints: Can't upload images as a background for individual blocks
workaround: No

goal:
When bulk tagging, the customer would like:
1) Notification when all bulk tags are complete
2) Notification on exactly how many contacts were tagged
constraints: I think there is supposed to be an email when a bulk tag is completed, but the client said they haven't been getting anything.
workaround: Have to check afterwards and see if the tag populated

goal: Customer would like to preview how their email looks in plain text without having to send a test email to themselves
constraints: Customer can't preview their email in plain text right now
workaround: Send a test email to themselves and open in plain text

goal: Easily look at daily SMS unsubs over time. If they are looking at `Contacts over time` on audience analytics, they would like to see the SMS unsub count for a specific day in their chart and legend.
constraints: Previously, the `Contacts over time` widget in Audience Analytics had a stacked bar that had subs + unsubs = total contacts. This allowed C3 to see the daily unsub count for a particular audience.
workaround: They can see the end of day unsubs in the `Contacts over time` widget by setting the date rage to the one day the C1 is reviewing. This is straightforward enough, but requires adjusting the date range for each day they are counting unsubs for.

goal:
The customer would like to be able to edit the unsubscribe form. As confirmed by the forms on-call team, the form that a recipient navigates to from a campaign (where their email address is automatically filled in the url) is not actually editable, so this form isn't reflected in the form builder, or anywhere in the UI to be edited.

However in the campaign itself, the form that shows is the "out-dated" form from the form-builder.
constraints: Disparity between form availability in the editor and the actual form the C2 sees when they unsubscribe. As well as inability to edit the form.
workaround: No

goal:
MYOB Email Service is an at-risk Tx-only user and the account is used as an 'email service' for Mandrill. So their C2s are triggering campaigns from their own MYOB accounts which get sent by Mandrill. 

Request for AI features to help identify and prevent deliverability & compliance issues in Mandrill/Transactional Email. Competitors such as Twilio offer this.
constraints: C1 is consistently flagged by compliance because of bad-sending - they can't control it because it's the C2s using bad practices.
workaround:
It was originally suggested to try setting up subaccounts for this, but this opens up another issue where there's just too many subaccounts to manage (a previous note that it may be ~5K C2s). 

It was also mentioned to maybe try setting up API/SFTP limits.  

The Compliance team had suggested maybe leveraging the rules engine, but as far as I can tell, it doesn't seem like it would prevent sending (per the listed actions).
